description: Transformation library using Option B (atomic clinical concepts) for DC concepts and AC Concept Model v0.16 for analysis concepts. Dimensions expressed through bindings with dataStructureRole. SmartPhrases compose into human-readable analysis descriptions.

mapping.bindings[]: The DSD (qb:DataStructureDefinition) of the transformation's input/output cubes. direction='input|output' distinguishes. dataStructureRole maps to qb:dimension | qb:measure | qb:attribute.
mapping.slices[]: qb:Slice (https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-data-cube/#slice) — fixed-dimension views of an input cube.
mapping.sliceKeys[]: qb:SliceKey (https://www.w3.org/TR/vocab-data-cube/#slice-key) — declares which dimensions are fixed in a slice.
mapping.bindings[].slice: AC/DC extension: pre-filter this binding's cube to the named qb:Slice before the method sees it.
mapping.bindings[].methodRole: AC/DC extension: maps a cube slot to the method's role (value, partition, minuend, subtrahend, result, …).
mapping.bindings[].conceptCategory: AC/DC extension: references a category (e.g. VisitDimension) rather than a concrete concept; the user picks a member at study-spec time.

roleDefinitions.description: Registry of SmartPhrase roles. Each role has a human-readable label, a display order, and a contextSource that classifies whether the role is typically supplied by an endpoint spec ('endpoint') or chosen directly by the user ('manual'). Runtime predicates that decide when a role is implicitly satisfied by a specific endpoint spec are app dynamics and live in ac-dc-app/data/phrase-role-config.json, not here.

derivationTransformations.name: Baseline Selection
derivationTransformations.description: Identify the baseline record per subject per parameter using M.RecordSelection configured for baseline.

bindings.note: RecordSelection operates on records, not values — no value type constraint

derivationTransformations.name: Change From Baseline
derivationTransformations.description: Calculate the arithmetic change from baseline value.
derivationTransformations.composedPhrase: change from baseline in {parameter} at {visit} with {imputation} imputation for missing data

bindings.note: Current visit value
Baseline-flagged record

derivationTransformations.name: Percent Change From Baseline
derivationTransformations.description: Calculate the percent change from baseline value.

bindings.note: Current visit value
Baseline-flagged record

derivationTransformations.name: Shift From Baseline
derivationTransformations.description: Determine categorical shift between baseline and post-baseline values.

bindings.note: Current visit categorical value
Baseline categorical value

derivationTransformations.name: Responder Classification
derivationTransformations.description: Classify subjects as responders based on threshold criterion applied to a derived value.

derivationTransformations.name: LOCF Imputation
derivationTransformations.description: Impute missing values by carrying forward the last non-missing observation within each subject-parameter partition, ordered by analysis visit.

bindings.note: LOCF operates on records — value type is pass-through

derivationTransformations.name: Analysis Visit Assignment
derivationTransformations.description: Assign records to analysis visit windows based on timing relative to protocol-defined encounters.

bindings.note: Window assignment uses study day (relative timing)

derivationTransformations.name: Population Flag Assignment
derivationTransformations.description: Assign subjects to analysis populations based on eligibility criteria.

bindings.note: CriterionEvaluation is criteria-dependent; the concept's valueType determines representation

derivationTransformations.name: Peak Concentration
derivationTransformations.description: Find the maximum concentration in an ordered PK profile.

derivationTransformations.name: Area Under Curve
derivationTransformations.description: Calculate the area under the concentration-time curve.

derivationTransformations.name: Overall Survival Time
derivationTransformations.description: Calculate time from randomisation to death or censoring.

bindings.note: Duration calculation uses absolute dates

derivationTransformations.name: Death Event Flag
derivationTransformations.description: Create censoring indicator for survival analysis (death = event, alive = censored).

derivationTransformations.name: ADAS-Cog Item Max Score
derivationTransformations.description: Max possible score for each ADAS-Cog item, used as scaling denominator when items are missing.

bindings.note: 11 ADAS-Cog item scores

derivationTransformations.name: ADAS-Cog Missing Scale Capacity
derivationTransformations.description: Sum of max-score capacity of missing items per subject/visit. Scaling factor = 70 / (70 - this value).

bindings.note: Max scores from T.ADAS_ItemMaxScore, filtered to missing items

derivationTransformations.name: ADAS-Cog Sum of Available Item Scores
derivationTransformations.description: Sum of available (non-missing) ADAS-Cog item scores per subject/visit.

bindings.note: 11 ADAS-Cog item scores (from BCs)

derivationTransformations.name: ADAS-Cog Non-Missing Item Count
derivationTransformations.description: Count of non-missing ADAS-Cog item scores per subject/visit. Used to determine if enough items are present for scoring.

bindings.note: 11 ADAS-Cog item scores (from BCs)

derivationTransformations.name: ADAS-Cog Scoreable Check
derivationTransformations.description: Flag whether enough items are present to compute total. Requires >= 8 of 11 items.

bindings.note: Count of non-missing items (M.Aggregation count)

derivationTransformations.name: ADAS-Cog (11) Total Score
derivationTransformations.description: total = sum_available / ((70 - missing_capacity) / 70). Missing if scoreable flag = N. Scale: 0-70.

bindings.note: Sum of available item scores (M.Aggregation sum)
Scale proportion from T.ADAS_MissingScaleCapacity
Scoreable flag from T.ADAS_ScoreableCheck

derivationTransformations.name: Unit Conversion
derivationTransformations.description: Convert a numeric Observation from its per-row source unit to a standardised target unit using the unit_conversions vocabulary. Generic — works for any numeric Observation whose BC carries a Result.Value + Result.Unit facet pair. Produces a derived Measure with both value and unit facets populated.

bindings.note: Source numeric value. Resolves via BC-to-OC instance mapping to the SDTM --ORRES variable (VSORRES for Height).
Source unit code varying per row. Resolves via BC-to-OC instance mapping to the SDTM --ORRESU variable (VSORRESU for Height).
Converted numeric value of the derived Measure.
Unit of the derived Measure — equals the target_unit configuration. Propagated so downstream derivations/analyses can read the Measure's unit without re-reading config.
BC identifier — resolves to --TESTCD in SDTM (e.g., VSTESTCD). The constraint value comes from the endpoint's linked BC specialization.

Output parameter label — value comes from the endpoint specification (Step 3), not from the source data. Replaces the SDTM Topic identifier with the clinically meaningful endpoint label (e.g., 'Weight (kg)').

methodConfigurations.description: Fail loudly if a factor is missing — CDISC convention prefers errors over silent NAs.

derivationTransformations.name: Derive Analysis Visit
derivationTransformations.description: Assign each observation to a scheduled analysis visit (AVISIT) via day-windows and a target-day tie-break. Default windows are for LZZT ADAS-Cog (≤1 Baseline, 2-84 Week 8, 85-140 Week 16, >140 Week 24). Consumes a pre-computed study day (SDTM --DY) relative to the randomization anchor BC.

bindings.note: Study day relative to the randomization anchor. In SDTM this is the --DY column (e.g. QSDY), computed by the load step as day-from-RFSTDTC.

methodConfigurations.description: BC providing the randomization anchor (Subject is Randomized, CDISC C114209). DSSTDTC is the per-subject anchor date; SDTM's --DY columns are already pre-computed relative to this. Override if your SAP uses a different anchor (e.g. First Dose BC).
Scheduled visit windows. Edit per SAP. dayMin/dayMax=null means unbounded on that side.
Tie-break: among windows matching a day, pick min |day−targetDay|, then min visitNumber.

analysisTransformations.name: Change From Baseline MMRM Primary Analysis
analysisTransformations.description: Primary efficacy analysis: MMRM on change from baseline, comparing treatment groups at each visit with baseline as covariate.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: change from baseline in {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population comparing {treatment} groups using mixed model repeated measures adjusting for baseline {parameter} with {conf_level}% confidence intervals

exampleInstance.resolvedPhrase: change from baseline in SBP (mmHg) at Week 24 in the ITT population comparing Drug A vs Placebo groups using mixed model repeated measures adjusting for baseline SBP (mmHg) with 95% confidence intervals

bindings.description: Baseline value of the endpoint parameter

analysisTransformations.name: Change From Baseline ANCOVA
analysisTransformations.description: ANCOVA on change from baseline at a specific visit, comparing treatment groups with baseline as covariate.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: change from baseline in {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population comparing {treatment} groups using analysis of covariance adjusting for baseline {parameter} and site with {conf_level}% confidence intervals

bindings.description: Baseline value of the endpoint parameter

analysisTransformations.name: Overall Survival Log-Rank Test
analysisTransformations.description: Compare overall survival between treatment groups using the log-rank test.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: time to death in the {population} population comparing {treatment} groups using the log-rank test

exampleInstance.resolvedPhrase: time to death in the ITT population comparing Drug A vs Placebo groups using the log-rank test

analysisTransformations.name: Descriptive Statistics of Change From Baseline
analysisTransformations.description: Summary statistics (N, mean, SD, median, Q1, Q3, min, max) for change from baseline by treatment group and visit.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: change from baseline in {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population comparing {treatment} groups summarised by descriptive statistics

analysisTransformations.name: Count (n)
analysisTransformations.description: Count of non-missing values for a measure by treatment group and visit.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: count of {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population by {treatment} group

analysisTransformations.name: Mean
analysisTransformations.description: Arithmetic mean of a measure by treatment group and visit.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: mean of {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population by {treatment} group

analysisTransformations.name: Standard Deviation
analysisTransformations.description: Standard deviation of a measure by treatment group and visit.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: standard deviation of {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population by {treatment} group

analysisTransformations.name: Median
analysisTransformations.description: Median of a measure by treatment group and visit.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: median of {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population by {treatment} group

analysisTransformations.name: Quartiles (Q1, Q3)
analysisTransformations.description: First and third quartiles of a measure by treatment group and visit.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: quartiles of {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population by {treatment} group

analysisTransformations.name: Minimum
analysisTransformations.description: Minimum value of a measure by treatment group and visit.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: minimum of {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population by {treatment} group

analysisTransformations.name: Maximum
analysisTransformations.description: Maximum value of a measure by treatment group and visit.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: maximum of {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population by {treatment} group

analysisTransformations.name: Responder Analysis Chi-Square
analysisTransformations.description: Compare responder proportions between treatment groups using chi-square test.
analysisTransformations.composedPhrase: proportion of responders in {parameter} at {visit} in the {population} population comparing {treatment} groups using the chi-square test
